channel.title: Craft
channel.description: Craft is a list of hand oriented craft and technology Do we really need all those power tools?

item.title: The Splinter Report: June 5th
item.description:
Welcome to The Splinter Report, where I gather the assorted interesting and amusing things I’ve seen in the woodworking world. This week we’re looking at a trash bin, an ottoman,...

Source

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/editors-blog/the-splinter-report-june-5th/)

item.title: Westone Thunder I Bass Restoration
item.description:
As you’ll know, once in a while, I like to do resto project: just for fun really! I’d been hankering for a Westone bass made in the legendary Matsumoku factory and I finally managed to get my hands on one. So, this series of videos is all about bring a 44 year old bass back to life!

Part 1


Part 2


Part 3
item.source:markdown:
As you’ll know, once in a while, I like to do resto project: just for fun really! I’d been hankering for a Westone bass made in the legendary Matsumoku factory and I finally managed to get my hands on one. So, this series of videos is all about bring a 44 year old bass back to life!
Part 1
Part 2
Part 3
item.author: noreply@blogger.com (Gary Nava, Luthier)

item.title: Jack-Be-Nimble
item.description:
These two new jacks do everything the older ones do but incorporate some welcome clamp characteristics. A year or two ago I talked about some new jacks not intended for...

Source

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/toolreviews/jack-be-nimble/)

item.title: Flip-Top Tool Cart
item.description:
No more lifting your heavy tools like mortisers. To store it, just tip it over. When I make mortises, my benchtop mortiser is the go-to tool because it’s fast and...

Source
item.source:markdown:

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/projects/self-storing-mortiser-cabinet/)

item.title: Dust Collector Trap Door
item.description:
Emptying my dust collector has never been something I looked forward to. For years I wrestled with the unit to empty the contents. First, I’d remove the bag and empty...

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/tricks/dust-collector-trap-door/)

item.title: New Fulfillment Center for Lost Art Press
item.description: This week we will move our order-fulfillment operation and warehouse to a nearby structure with more than 8,000 square feet of space, plus a loading dock and dedicated space for tool assembly and production of glue and our soft wax finish. So if you place an order this week, it might be delayed several days...
item.source:markdown: This week we will move our order-fulfillment operation and warehouse to a nearby structure with more than 8,000 square feet of space, plus a loading dock and dedicated space for tool assembly and production of glue and our soft wax finish. So if you place an order this week, it might be delayed several days...

item.title: Kubb
item.description:
An outdoor game that’s part bowling, part horseshoes, part chess—and totally fun for all ages.  If you enjoy picnics by playing a relaxing lawn game with the family, you’re going...

item.title: Frank Klausz Finger Joint How-to [Video]
item.description:
It’s hard to fathom, but finger joints are a relatively new joinery method for Frank Klausz, because why not just cut dovetails when you need a box?  In fact, when...

Source

item.title: Gel Varnish
item.description:
The (almost) perfect compromise. Most woodworkers do their finishing with one of two wipe-on/wipe-off finishes: oil/varnish blend or wiping varnish. Oil/varnish blend is a thinned mixture of boiled linseed oil...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2020/01/02101911/0912-ff-3-1_thickness-1-1024x766.jpg)The (almost) perfect compromise. Most woodworkers do their finishing with one of two wipe-on/wipe-off finishes: oil/varnish blend or wiping varnish. Oil/varnish blend is a thinned mixture of boiled linseed oil...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/finishing/gel-varnish/)

item.title: Post and Rung Chairs
item.description:
Slowly removing the barriers to start building. Post and rung chairs can seem intimidating to many: the angles, the steam bending, and the seat weaving. However, this iconic Appalachian style...

Source
item.source:markdown:

Post and rung chairs can seem intimidating to many: the angles, the steam bending, and the seat weaving. However, this iconic Appalachian style...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/editors-blog/post-and-rung-chairs/)

item.title: How to Install Butt Hinges
item.description: When I can’t afford the fancy forged hinges (or would rather spend that money elsewhere), I most often reach for 2-1/2″ butt hinges from Horton Brasses (item PB-409, about $42 per pair, including screws and shipping). And while butt hinges are available at any hardware store and at a range of price points (some incredibly...
item.source:markdown: When I can’t afford the fancy forged hinges (or would rather spend that money elsewhere), I most often reach for 2-1/2″ butt hinges from Horton Brasses (item PB-409, about $42 per pair, including screws and shipping). And while butt hinges are available at any hardware store and at a range of price points (some incredibly...

item.title: Old Growth, Modern Treasure
item.description:
At St. Albans Cathedral, the old Norman construction is incredible, but the woodworking is out of this world. I’m always impressed by old-growth wood and things made with it. Creating something...

I’m always impressed by old-growth wood and things made with it. Creating something...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/editors-blog/old-growth-modern-treasure/)

item.title: 114. Thierry André
item.description:
Working out of his solo shop in Quebec, Thierry André is a builder of truly one-of-a-kind instruments.

For episode 114 of the podcast, Thierry dives into his design process and explains why he believes every instrument should include an element of danger and surprise. We also talk about his formative time apprenticing with Fred Carlson and much more.

GoFundMe to help Thierry Andre beat cancer: https://www.gofundme.com/f/help-thierry-andre-beat-cancer
 
Links: https://www.thierryandre.com
https://www.instagram.com/thierryandre.studio/
 
Luthier on Luthier is hosted by Michael Bashkin of Bashkin Guitars and brought to you by the Fretboard Journal. This episode is sponsored by the Looth Group, Dream Guitars and StewMac.
item.source:markdown:
Working out of his solo shop in Quebec, Thierry André is a builder of truly one-of-a-kind instruments.
For episode 114 of the podcast, Thierry dives into his design process and explains why he believes every instrument should include an element of danger and surprise. We also talk about his formative time apprenticing with Fred Carlson and much more.
GoFundMe to help Thierry Andre beat cancer: [https://www.gofundme.com/f/help-thierry-andre-beat-cancer](https://www.gofundme.com/f/help-thierry-andre-beat-cancer)
Links: [https://www.thierryandre.com](https://www.thierryandre.com)
[https://www.instagram.com/thierryandre.studio/](https://www.instagram.com/thierryandre.studio/)
Luthier on Luthier is hosted by Michael Bashkin of [Bashkin Guitars](https://www.bashkinguitars.com) and brought to you by the [Fretboard Journal](https://shop.fretboardjournal.com/products/fretboard-journal-annual-subscription). This episode is sponsored by [the Looth Group](https://loothgroup.com), [Dream Guitars](https://www.dreamguitars.com/) and [StewMac](https://www.stewmac.com/?irclickid=VA-TmuXZ%3AxyPUn0Ut-05ZTupUkHUPAzGE2bmy00&utm_source=3755630&utm_medium=Impact&utm_campaign=3755630&utm_content=Online%20Tracking%20Link_1303370&irgwc=1&partner=Fretboard%20Journal&mpid=3755630&group=).

item.title: Social-impact Luthiery
item.description: More than 150 years ago, in the heart of Appalachia, James Edward Thomas, known as Uncle Ed, loaded up a handcart and pushed it along the banks of Troublesome Creek, selling his handmade mountain dulcimers in Hindman, Kentucky. Today, at Troublesome Creek Stringed Instrument Co., the making of these same dulcimers, as  well as guitars,...
item.source:markdown: More than 150 years ago, in the heart of Appalachia, James Edward Thomas, known as Uncle Ed, loaded up a handcart and pushed it along the banks of Troublesome Creek, selling his handmade mountain dulcimers in Hindman, Kentucky. Today, at Troublesome Creek Stringed Instrument Co., the making of these same dulcimers, as well as guitars,...

item.title: ShopNotes Podcast Episode 273 — Free Lumber! You Fell, You Haul, You Mill (Don’t Mess Up My Yard)

item.title: Closed for Memorial Day
item.description: A reminder that the Lost Art Press storefront is closed today for Memorial Day. We’ll be back to regular hours (10 a.m.-2 p.m., Mon., Tue., Wed. & Fri.) as of tomorrow.
item.source:markdown: A reminder that the Lost Art Press storefront is closed today for Memorial Day. We’ll be back to regular hours (10 a.m.-2 p.m., Mon., Tue., Wed. & Fri.) as of tomorrow.

item.title: French Polishing Myths
item.description:

item.title: Registration is Open for London Classes
item.description: Chris and I are once again headed to London to teach classes with the London International Woodworking Festival at the end of October. Chris is teaching a four-day (Oct. 26-29) Irish Chair Class, and a short class (Oct. 30) on Peasant Carving, and I am (no surprise) teaching a four-day (Oct. 26-29) Dutch Tool Chest...
item.source:markdown: Chris and I are once again headed to London to teach classes with the London International Woodworking Festival at the end of October. Chris is teaching a four-day (Oct. 26-29) Irish Chair Class, and a short class (Oct. 30) on Peasant Carving, and I am (no surprise) teaching a four-day (Oct. 26-29) Dutch Tool Chest...

item.description:

item.title: Lego Workbenches
item.description: Last week, we received a treat of an email from Douglas Lambert, who recreated three workbenches from “Workbenches: From Design & Theory to Construction & Use” by Christopher Schwarz* in Lego form, and sent us detailed plans for each: “The Nicholson”  “Le Roubo” “Ye Knockdown Nicholson” You can download the plans for free at each...
item.source:markdown: Last week, we received a treat of an email from Douglas Lambert, who recreated three workbenches from “Workbenches: From Design & Theory to Construction & Use” by Christopher Schwarz\* in Lego form, and sent us detailed plans for each: “The Nicholson” “Le Roubo” “Ye Knockdown Nicholson” You can download the plans for free at each...

item.title: LAP Storefront Closed on Memorial Day
item.description: The LAP editorial staff is happy to welcome to the Lost Art Press storefront (407 Madison Ave., Covington, Ky., 41011) from 10 a.m.-2 p.m. on most Mondays, Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Fridays. But a heads up that we will be closed on Monday, May 25, for Memorial Day.
item.source:markdown: The LAP editorial staff is happy to welcome to the Lost Art Press storefront (407 Madison Ave., Covington, Ky., 41011) from 10 a.m.-2 p.m. on most Mondays, Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Fridays. But a heads up that we will be closed on Monday, May 25, for Memorial Day.

item.title: Perfect Sharpening Angle Every Time
item.description:
Chisels and planes have bevel angles from 25 to 35 degrees depending on their intended use. You can buy a jig for grinding these angles, or make your own angle...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2026/05/19094643/oct00-right-angle-every-time-copy-1024x768.jpg)Chisels and planes have bevel angles from 25 to 35 degrees depending on their intended use. You can buy a jig for grinding these angles, or make your own angle...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/tricks/perfect-sharpening-angle-every-time/)

item.title: Mind Upon Mind: Another Way to Use the Stanley No. 77 Doweling Machine
item.description: Editor’s note: Our Mind Upon Mind series is a nod to a 1937 Chips from the Chisel column (also featured in “Honest Labour: The Charles H. Hayward Years”), in which Hayward wrote, “The influence of mind upon mind is extraordinary.” The idea being there’s often room for improvement. To that end, we’ve asked you what else you have...
item.source:markdown: Editor’s note: Our Mind Upon Mind series is a nod to a 1937 Chips from the Chisel column (also featured in “Honest Labour: The Charles H. Hayward Years”), in which Hayward wrote, “The influence of mind upon mind is extraordinary.” The idea being there’s often room for improvement. To that end, we’ve asked you what else you have...

item.title: ShopNotes Podcast Episode 272 — What’s Your Relationship Like with Your Hardwood Supplier?
